descriptions: Aryan Nations was founded as the paramilitary wing of the Church of Jesus Christ–Christian.
edges.evidence_quote: After moving to Idaho, Butler founded Aryan Nations as a paramilitary wing of the church

descriptions: The Yau Lai split from the Wah Ching.
edges.evidence_quote: The Yau Lai (also known as the Yo Le or Yau Lay, meaning 'good fortune') split from the Wah Ching in 1969

descriptions: The Joe Boys originated as a splinter from the Yau Lai.
edges.evidence_quote: Joe Fong's group had splintered from the main Yau Lai in early 1971

descriptions: Barriox13 has conflicts with CV117st.
edges.evidence_quote: Barriox13 remains active in its original territory and maintains conflicts with other Hispanic gangs, including CV155st, CV Largo36, CV117st, and others from Compton and South Central.

descriptions: Listed as allies in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Orchard Park Trailblazers , Heath Street Gang, Academy Homes Gang, Villa Victoria Park Gang , Everton Geneva Grizzleys

descriptions: The Mancuso have contacts with the Torcasio-Giampà group.

edges.evidence_quote: nel lametino hanno contatti con il gruppo Torcasio - Giampà

edges.evidence_quote: contacts with the Torcasio-Giampà group

7th Street Gang.description: The 7th Street Gang was a Hispanic street gang based in Buffalo, New York, founded in 2000 by Efrain "Cheko" Hidalgo. It engaged in drug trafficking of cocaine, heroin, and other narcotics, and was involved in violent conflicts with rival gangs, particularly the 10th Street Gang. The gang was active from 2000 to 2017, when many members were arrested.

descriptions: The 10th Street Gang had a rivalry with the 7th Street Gang, leading to violence and homicides.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang started garnering infamy when they started having rivalries with a newly established gang, the 7th Street gang.

descriptions: The 7th Street Gang and 10th Street Gang were rivals, with violence between them.
edges.evidence_quote: They were heated rivals of the 10th street gang.

descriptions: The infobox lists Latin Kings as allies of the 7th Street Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Latin Kings

descriptions: Bones MC German chapters merged into Hells Angels MC via patchover.
edges.evidence_quote: patchover of the also formerly Mannheim-based German chapters of the Bones MC to the Hells Angels MC in 1999

descriptions: Gremium became the largest German MC after Bones MC chapters patched over to Hells Angels.
edges.evidence_quote: Gremium claims to be the largest German motorcycle club since the patchover of the also formerly Mannheim-based German chapters of the Bones MC to the Hells Angels MC in 1999

descriptions: Both clubs participated together in the 1947 Hollister incident.
edges.evidence_quote: The Market Street Commandos was an outlaw motorcycle club that, in 1947, along with the Boozefighters and the Pissed Off Bastards of Bloomington , participated in the highly publicized Hollister incident

descriptions: POBOB and Boozefighters jointly participated in the 1947 Hollister riot.
edges.evidence_quote: The Pissed Off Bastards of Bloomington (POBOB) is a motorcycle club that, in 1947, along with the Boozefighters and the Market Street Commandos , participated in the highly publicized Hollister riot

descriptions: The Boozefighters were in talks to patch over into the Warlocks.
edges.evidence_quote: as well as in talks with the Boozefighters about patching over into the Warlocks

descriptions: Masaki-gumi disbanded from Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi.
edges.evidence_quote: Masaki-gumi ... disbanded

descriptions: The Abbruzzese clan tried to involve the families of Cirò in their conflict with Cosenza.
edges.evidence_quote: Di conseguenza il clan degli zingari tentò di coinvolgere le famiglie di Cirò e Corigliano Calabro e far ricadere la colpa su Cosenza.

descriptions: The Mau Maus were formed by members who broke away from the Apaches.
edges.evidence_quote: Some members of a street gang called the Apaches broke away and created the Mau Maus

descriptions: IS lists Syrian Resistance as a non-state opponent, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Other non-state opponents: ... Syrian Resistance – Suqur al-Furat

descriptions: The Tavoletta-Ucciero and Schiavone-Bidognetti families were in conflict within the Casalesi clan.
edges.evidence_quote: terza faida interna ai Casalesi : combattuta dal 2003 al 2007 tra le famiglie Tavoletta-Ucciero e Schiavone-Bidognetti.

descriptions: Bandidos and Rogues Gallery engaged in a shootout.
edges.evidence_quote: In October 1999, a Bandidos member and a hangaround were involved in a shootout in Lahti with a member of the Rogues Gallery gang who suffered a leg wound

descriptions: Bandidos members shot and wounded a Rogues Gallery member.
edges.evidence_quote: Bandidos Helsinki chapter president Pentti Tapio Haapanen and vice-president Harri Tapio Reinikainen were convicted of shooting and wounding a Rogues Gallery member and a member of the Lepakko Gang in Sörnäinen, Helsinki in July 2005.

descriptions: Rogues Gallery was founded by former Cannonball members from the Lahti chapter.
edges.evidence_quote: The former members of this chapter then founded the Rogues Gallery gang in the city in January 2000.

descriptions: Ping On and Hung Mong were allies in the late 1980s.
edges.evidence_quote: The organization had several allies in the late 1980s, including the Hung Mong which was in operation in New York City.

descriptions: Ping On and Hung Mong are listed as allies in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: 14K , Hung Mong

descriptions: The Zito clan allied with the De Stefano during the war.
edges.evidence_quote: per i De Stefano capeggiati ora da Orazio , fratello di Paolo ci sono i Libri , i Tegano , i Latella , i Barreca , i Paviglianiti e gli Zito.

descriptions: Lo Presti formed alliances with the Zito 'ndrina in Canada.
edges.evidence_quote: Ha avuto rapporti di affari con la Famiglia Inzerillo -Spatola-Gambino di Palermo , attraverso la quale strinse alleanze anche oltreoceano con la Famiglia Gambino di New York e con le famiglie della 'Ndrangheta in Canada degli Zito , dei Commisso , dei Racco, degli Stalteri a Toronto e dei Cotroni a Montréal .

descriptions: Aston Villa Hardcore succeeded the C-Crew as a hooligan firm.
edges.evidence_quote: The firm have been active since circa 1993, following on from previous Aston Villa hooligan firms the Steamers, C-Crew and Villa Youth.

Ñetas.description: The Ñetas are a criminal organization originally formed among inmates at Oso Blanco prison in Río Piedras, Puerto Rico, in 1979 by Carlos Torres Iriarte. Initially a mutual protection group, they evolved into a dominant prison gang involved in drug trafficking, robbery, and murder. They have expanded to the United States and Spain, with an estimated 7,000 members in Puerto Rico and 5,000 abroad. The group remains active and is known for its white color identification.

descriptions: Big Circle Gang lists Netas as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: The Company, American Mafia, Red Wa, Fujian gangs, Wo Shing Wo, Menace of Destruction, Asian Boyz, MS-13, Netas

descriptions: Ñetas is listed as a rival of Latin Kings.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Crips, Dominicans Don't Play, Folk Nation, Gangster Disciples, Latin Eagles, Maniac Latin Disciples, MS-13, Ñetas, La Raza Nation, Simon City Royals, Los Solidos, Spanish Cobras, Tiny Rascal Gang, United Blood Nation

descriptions: The Ñetas are the main rivals of the Latin Kings in Spain.
edges.evidence_quote: In Spain, the Ñetas are the main rivals of the Latin Kings.

descriptions: Latin Kings and Ñetas were in conflict in Ecuador but reached a peace agreement in 2009.
edges.evidence_quote: los Latin Kings y los Ñetas se introdujeron en Ecuador desde España. En el país andino , tras pocos años de violenta actividad, ambas bandas llegaron a un acuerdo de paz (solo en Ecuador) y cese de actividades en 2009.

descriptions: Latin Kings and Ñetas reached a peace agreement in Ecuador in 2009.
edges.evidence_quote: los Latin Kings y los Ñetas se introdujeron en Ecuador desde España. En el país andino , tras pocos años de violenta actividad, ambas bandas llegaron a un acuerdo de paz (solo en Ecuador) y cese de actividades en 2009.

edges.evidence_quote: small connections with ... the Puerto Rican street gang Ñetas

descriptions: Trinitarios and Ñetas engaged in a brawl in Madrid in 2014.
edges.evidence_quote: a brawl occurred in Madrid involving Trinitarios and Ñetas , leading to the arrests of twenty-six gang members.

descriptions: UBN formed to protect against Ñetas.
edges.evidence_quote: UBN started in 1993 in Rikers Island's George Motchan Detention Center (GMDC) to form protection from the Latin Kings and Ñetas who were targeting African-American gang members.

descriptions: Infobox lists Boricua Popular Army as an ally.

edges.evidence_quote: La pandilla Ñetas se identifica con el Ejército Popular Boricua y su filosofía revolucionaria en la búsqueda de la independencia de Puerto Rico.

descriptions: Crips is listed as a rival of Ñetas.

descriptions: Ñetas were formed to fight Grupo 27, and they have a long-standing conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: They formed as a mutual protection group, ostensibly to improve living conditions and defend inmates from abuses committed by guards and other prisoners, as well as to fight the prison gang Grupo 27 ("Group 27"), or the "Insectos" ("Insects").

descriptions: Ñetas and Grupo 27 have a long-standing violent conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Los Ñetas se convirtieron en la pandilla dominante en la prisión de Oso Blanco a principios de la década de 1980, pero su conflicto con los G27 continuó.

descriptions: Infobox lists La Rompe ONU as a rival.

edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: 20 LUV, Dominicans Don't Play, Grupo 27, Latin Kings, La Rompe ONU, Los Solidos, Trinitarios, United Blood Nation

descriptions: Ñetas members killed Latin Kings members, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Jason "J-Live" Cabral, leader of the gang's Long Island faction, was sentenced on November 18, 2014, to a term of 37 years' imprisonment as a result of his guilty plea to the 2004 murders of Anthony Marcano and Fabian Mestres. The 17-year-old Marcano and Mestres, members of the Latin Kings, were robbed and killed after being lured to a house in Brentwood on August 10, 2004.

descriptions: Ñetas and Latin Kings are rivals in Spain.
edges.evidence_quote: Los principales rivales de la rama española de los Ñetas son los Latin Kings.

descriptions: Ñetas and Latin Kings reached a peace agreement in Ecuador in 2009.
edges.evidence_quote: En el país andino, tras pocos años de violenta actividad, ambas bandas llegaron a un acuerdo de paz (solo en Ecuador) y cese de actividades en 2009.

descriptions: Infobox lists Los Solidos as a rival.

descriptions: Ñetas members killed MS-13 members, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: On June 3, 2003, seven leaders of the Ñeta Association were arrested in Long Island, New York, for the stabbing and murder of two MS-13 members.

descriptions: Ñetas members killed MS-13 members.
edges.evidence_quote: siete líderes de la Asociación Ñeta fueron arrestados en Long Island, Nueva York, por el apuñalamiento y asesinato de dos miembros de la MS-13.

Kerryonians.description: The Kerryonians were an Irish-American street gang based in the Five Points area of New York City, active from the 1820s to the 1830s. Founded by recent immigrants from County Kerry, Ireland, they were among the earliest criminal gangs in the city, known for targeting British-descended New Yorkers and disrupting a performance by actor William Charles Macready in 1849. The gang was eventually absorbed into larger Five Points gangs such as the Dead Rabbits, Roach Guards, and Chichesters.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Chichesters, Tammany Hall, Plug Uglies, Roach Guards, Mulberry Street Boys, Municipal Police, Forty Thieves, Shirt Tails, Kerryonians
